assumptions: The dividend receivable and corresponding payable arise between entities within the same consolidated group.
The identified exposure is foreign currency risk arising on an intragroup balance or transaction reflected in the consolidation analysis.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge accounting as one of the three top-level hedge accounting models for recognised assets or liabilities exposed to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge accounting as a top-level hedge accounting model for variability in cash flows, including from recognised items or highly probable forecast transactions.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge accounting for a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct top-level hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements.
